data.description: The 38-0 football game challenges fans to draft a Premier League squad and simulate a perfect season with 38 wins from 38 matches.

}</style><script>window.grvInitialQuery = window.location.search;</script><script>    /**
     * TEMPORARY WORKAROUND: Detect if GRV Identity converted hash params to query params
     * This runs early to redirect before page fully loads
     * IMPORTANT: Preserve GRV Identity query params (signin, code, etc.) during conversion
     */
    (function() {
        try {
            const url = new URL(window.location.href);
            const hasTpopen = url.searchParams.has('tpopen');
            const hasTp = url.searchParams.has('tp');
            const hasCommentID = url.searchParams.has('commentID');

            // If we have TalkingPoints query params, convert them to hash and redirect
            if (hasTpopen || (hasTp && hasCommentID)) {
                const tpopen = url.searchParams.get('tpopen');
                const tp = url.searchParams.get('tp');
                const commentID = url.searchParams.get('commentID');

                // Build hash params
                let hashParams = [];
                if (tpopen) hashParams.push('tpopen=' + encodeURIComponent(tpopen));
                if (tp) hashParams.push('tp=' + encodeURIComponent(tp));
                if (commentID) hashParams.push('commentID=' + encodeURIComponent(commentID));

                if (hashParams.length > 0) {
                    // Remove ONLY TalkingPoints params from query string
                    // PRESERVE other query params like ?signin=success, ?code=, etc.
                    url.searchParams.delete('tpopen');
                    url.searchParams.delete('tp');
                    url.searchParams.delete('commentID');

                    // Build new URL with hash
                    let newUrl = url.origin + url.pathname;
                    // Preserve remaining query params (including GRV Identity params)
                    // IMPORTANT: Use url.searchParams.toString() to get updated search string after deletions
                    const remainingParams = url.searchParams.toString();
                    if (remainingParams) {
                        newUrl += '?' + remainingParams;
                    }
                    newUrl += '#' + hashParams.join('&');

                    // Redirect to hash-based URL immediately
                    if (window.history && window.history.replaceState) {
                        window.history.replaceState(null, '', newUrl);
                    } else {
                        window.location.replace(newUrl);
                    }
                }
            }
        } catch (e) {
            // Silent fail - redirect script error
        }
    })();

Build a Premier League squad, simulate a season and try to win every single league match.</p><p>That means 38 wins from 38 games, with no draws, no defeats and no room for error.</p><h2 class="wp-block-heading" id="what-is-the-380-football-game">What is the 38-0 football game?</h2><figure class="wp-block-image size-large"><div><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="682" loading="lazy" sizes="auto, (max-width: 991px) 100vw, 800px" src="https://cdn3.hitc.com/uploads/1/2026/06/GettyImages-2202548626-1024x682.jpg" alt="In this photo illustration, a Wordle logo seen displayed on" class="wp-image-2574555" srcset="https://cdn3.hitc.com/uploads/1/2026/06/GettyImages-2202548626-1024x682.jpg 1024w, https://cdn3.hitc.com/uploads/1/2026/06/GettyImages-2202548626-300x200.jpg 300w, https://cdn3.hitc.com/uploads/1/2026/06/GettyImages-2202548626-768x512.jpg 768w, https://cdn3.hitc.com/uploads/1/2026/06/GettyImages-2202548626-1536x1023.jpg 1536w"><div class="GRVAd" data-ad-type="inimage" data-ad-sizemapping="inimageDesktop" data-ad-inview="false" data-ad-status="loaded" data-ad-code="3612"><div id="div-gpt-ad-3612-0"></div></div></div><figcaption title="POLAND - 2025/03/02: In this photo illustration, a Wordle logo seen displayed on a smartphone. (Photo Illustration by Mateusz Slodkowski/SOPA Images/LightRocket via Getty Images)">Photo Illustration by Mateusz Slodkowski/SOPA Images/LightRocket via Getty Images</figcaption></figure><p>The <a href="https://www.balls.ie/football/footballs-version-of-wordle-the-addictive-38-0-game-sweeping-social-media-explained-656721" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener nofollow">38-0 football game</a> is a squad-building challenge focused on creating a Premier League team through a random draft.</p><div class="GRVMpuWrapper mobile"><div class="GRVAd inline mobile" data-ad-type="primary-mpu" data-ad-label="mpu" data-ad-sizemapping="primary-mpu" data-ad-sizes="[[300,250],[300,600],[320, 480]]"></div></div><p>Players are presented with options from different eras, allowing teams to mix modern stars, past legends and cult favourites.</p><p>The aim is to simulate a perfect Premier League season, recording 38 wins from 38 matches.</p><p>That is why the game has spread so quickly. It takes one of football’s most unrealistic goals and turns it into a quick, repeatable online challenge.</p><p>The Wordle comparison is not exact, but it fits. The game is easy to pick up, fast to play and simple to share.</p><div class="separator carousel-sep" data-txt="▼▼   Article Continues Below   ▼▼"></div><div class="carousel-title title"> <div class="hf">MORE FIFA WORLD CUP 2026 STORIES</div> <span></span> </div><div class="carousel carousel-js c-has-mpu" data-mode="post"> <div> <div class="c-buttons hide"> <div class="prev hide"><svg width="20" height="20"><use href="#list-arrow-svg"></use></svg></div> <div class="next"><svg width="20" height="20"><use href="#list-arrow-svg"></use></svg></div> </div> <div class="post-list c-list load-list c-list-js" data-url="https://www.hitc.com/json/v2/posts/carousel?term=62394" data-internal="1" data-mode="carousel"> <svg class="loading" width="118" height="36" viewBox="0 0 118 36"><use href="#dots-svg"></use></svg><div class="c-ad c-desktop"><div class="GRVMpuWrapper"><div class="GRVAd inline-ad" data-ad-type="carousel" data-ad-sizes="[[300,250]]" data-ad-status="waiting" data-ad-code="90796"><div id="div-gpt-ad-90796-0"></div></div></div></div></div> </div><div class="c-ad c-mobile"><div class="GRVMpuWrapper"><div class="GRVAd inline-ad" data-ad-type="carousel" data-ad-sizes="[[300,250]]"></div></div></div></div><p>It also has the same frustration loop. A close call makes the next attempt feel necessary rather than optional.</p><h2 class="wp-block-heading" id="how-do-you-play-380-and-what-is-the-best-strategy">How do you play 38-0 and what is the best strategy?</h2><p>To play 38-0, users draft a Premier League squad from random options before simulating a season.</p><p>The game is available on the PL XI 38-0 Perfect Cup page, where the goal is to build a side strong enough to go through an entire campaign without drawing or losing.</p><div class="GRVMpuWrapper mobile"><div class="GRVAd inline mobile" data-ad-type="mpu" data-ad-sizes="[[300,250]]" data-ad-status="waiting" data-ad-code="75176"><div id="div-gpt-ad-75176-0"></div></div></div><p>Matches are simulated quickly, and a full season can be completed in minutes.</p><p>Success is not just about picking the biggest attacking names. Many players struggle because they draft high-rated forwards early and end up short in defence.</p><p>There are usually more elite wingers and strikers available later on, so it often makes sense to fill defensive positions first.</p><p>Other recommended settings include playing on easy mode, displaying prime ratings and using all-time Premier League rosters.</p><p>Still, nothing guarantees a perfect season. Even the strongest squads can fall short.</p><div class="GRVMpuWrapper mobile"><div class="GRVAd inline mobile" data-ad-type="mpu" data-ad-sizes="[[300,250]]"></div></div><p>There is also a Championship version called 46-0, which extends the challenge across a longer second-tier season.</p><p>In 46-0, players can simulate each match individually and make signings during the January transfer window.</p><p>It is a simple formula, but it works. A clear target, quick results and just enough frustration to make one more attempt feel worthwhile.</p><div class="wp-block-grvmedia-grv-related-articles"><div><strong>Read more:</strong></div><ul #tp-consent-overlay{position:fixed;top:0;left:0;width:100%;height:100%;background:rgba(0,0,0,.5);z-index:99999;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center}
        #tp-consent-overlay .tp-cc{background:#fff;color:#000;border-radius:14px;padding:24px;max-width:400px;width:90%;box-shadow:0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,.25);position:relative;font-family:sans-serif}
        #tp-consent-overlay .tp-cx{position:absolute;top:10px;right:14px;background:none;border:none;font-size:24px;cursor:pointer;line-height:1;color:#888}
        #tp-consent-overlay h3{margin:0 0 12px;font-size:1.1em}
        #tp-consent-overlay p{margin:0 0 20px;font-size:.95em;line-height:1.5}
        #tp-consent-overlay .tp-ca{display:flex;gap:10px;justify-content:flex-end}
        #tp-consent-overlay button[data-tp=no]{padding:8px 16px;border:1px solid #ccc;border-radius:6px;background:#fff;cursor:pointer;font-size:.9em}
        #tp-consent-overlay button[data-tp=yes]{padding:8px 16px;border:none;border-radius:6px;background:#000;color:#fff;cursor:pointer;font-size:.9em}
    </style> <script>        /**
         * TEMPORARY WORKAROUND: Hash-based URL conversion and sidebar opening
         *
         * This script converts TalkingPoints URLs from query parameters (?tpopen=true&tp=slug)
         * to hash-based URLs (#tpopen=true&tp=slug) and handles opening the sidebar.
         *
         * TODO: Move this functionality into the Vue app:
         *   1. Update vue-src/src/utils/url.ts - getShareUrl() to generate hash URLs
         *   2. Update Vue app initialization to read hash params in addition to query params
         *   3. Remove this entire script block once Vue app is updated and rebuilt
         */
        (function() {
            if (typeof navigator === 'undefined') return;

            // PART 1: Intercept share/copy actions to convert URLs to hash format
            const originalShare = navigator.share ? navigator.share.bind(navigator) : null;
            const originalWriteText = navigator.clipboard ? navigator.clipboard.writeText.bind(navigator.clipboard) : null;

            function convertToHashUrl(url) {
                try {
                    const urlObj = new URL(url);
                    const tpopen = urlObj.searchParams.get('tpopen');
                    const tp = urlObj.searchParams.get('tp');
                    const commentID = urlObj.searchParams.get('commentID');

                    if (!tp) return url;

                    let base = urlObj.origin + urlObj.pathname;
                    let hashParams = [];

                    if (tpopen) hashParams.push('tpopen=' + encodeURIComponent(tpopen));
                    if (tp) hashParams.push('tp=' + encodeURIComponent(tp));
                    if (commentID) hashParams.push('commentID=' + encodeURIComponent(commentID));

                    return base + '#' + hashParams.join('&');
                } catch (e) {
                    return url;
                }
            }

            if (originalShare) {
                navigator.share = function(data) {
                    if (data && data.url) data.url = convertToHashUrl(data.url);
                    return originalShare(data);
                };
            }

            if (originalWriteText && navigator.clipboard) {
                navigator.clipboard.writeText = function(text) {
                    if (text && (text.includes('tpopen=true') || text.includes('tp='))) {
                        text = convertToHashUrl(text);
                    }
                    return originalWriteText(text);
                };
            }

            // PART 2: Intercept link clicks for TalkingPoints comment navigation
            // NOTE: Skip this on /talkingpoints/ template page as it has its own handler
            document.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
                // Don't intercept on the template page - it has its own handler
                if (window.location.pathname === '/talkingpoints/' || window.location.pathname === '/talkingpoints') {
                    return;
                }

                const link = e.target.closest('a');
                if (!link || !link.href) return;

                const href = link.href;

                // FIRST: Intercept OLD format links (article URLs with #tp= hash)
                // These are legacy links that need to redirect to new /talkingpoints/slug format
                if (href.includes('#tp=') && !href.includes('/talkingpoints/')) {
                    try {
                        e.preventDefault();
                        e.stopPropagation();

                        const urlObj = new URL(href);
                        const hashParams = new URLSearchParams(urlObj.hash.substring(1));
                        const tpSlug = hashParams.get('tp');
                        const commentID = hashParams.get('commentID') || hashParams.get('commentId');

                        if (tpSlug) {
                            // Redirect to new format: /talkingpoints/slug#commentID=id
                            let newUrl = window.location.origin + '/talkingpoints/' + tpSlug;
                            if (commentID) {
                                newUrl += '#commentID=' + encodeURIComponent(commentID);
                            }
                            window.location.href = newUrl;
                        }
                        return;
                    } catch (err) {
                        // Invalid URL, let default behavior happen
                    }
                }

                // SECOND: Check if this is a TalkingPoints story URL with hash params
                if (href.includes('/talkingpoints/') && (href.includes('#tp=') || href.includes('#commentID='))) {
                    try {
                        const urlObj = new URL(href);
                        const hashParams = new URLSearchParams(urlObj.hash.substring(1));
                        const commentID = hashParams.get('commentID');
                        const tpSlug = hashParams.get('tp');
                        const currentPath = window.location.pathname;
                        const linkPath = urlObj.pathname;

                        // Check if we're already on the target story page
                        if (currentPath === linkPath && commentID) {
                            // Same page - just scroll to comment without reloading
                            e.preventDefault();
                            e.stopPropagation();

                            window.location.hash = 'commentID=' + encodeURIComponent(commentID);

                            // Wait for comment to be rendered, then scroll with retry
                            let scrollAttempts = 0;
                            const maxScrollAttempts = 30;

                            function tryScrollToComment() {
                                const commentElement = document.getElementById(commentID);
                                if (commentElement) {
                                    commentElement.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'center' });
                                } else if (scrollAttempts < maxScrollAttempts) {
                                    scrollAttempts++;
                                    setTimeout(tryScrollToComment, 100);
                                }
                            }

                            setTimeout(tryScrollToComment, 200);
                        }
                        // If different page, let the link navigate normally
                    } catch (err) {
                        // Invalid URL, let default behavior happen
                    }
                }
            }, true);

            // Consent helpers for GRVConsentQueue push sites
            window._tpShowConsentModal = function(detail, onGranted) {
                var KEY = 'consent_sidebar';
                var existing = null;
                try { existing = localStorage.getItem(KEY); } catch(e) {}

                if (existing === 'granted') { onGranted(); return; }
                if (document.getElementById('tp-consent-overlay')) { return; }

                var overlay = document.createElement('div');
                overlay.id = 'tp-consent-overlay';
                overlay.innerHTML = '<div class="tp-cc">'
                    + '<button data-tp="close" class="tp-cx">×</button>'
                    + '<h3>Third-party scripts required</h3>'
                    + '<p>Opening the sidebar will load third-party scripts including Coral for comments. Do you want to continue?</p>'
                    + '<div class="tp-ca">'
                    + '<button data-tp="no">No thanks</button>'
                    + '<button data-tp="yes">Yes, proceed</button>'
                    + '</div></div>';

                function dismiss(granted) {
                    try { localStorage.setItem(KEY, granted ? 'granted' : 'denied'); } catch(e) {}
                    document.body.removeChild(overlay);
                    if (granted) onGranted();
                }

                overlay.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
                    var tp = e.target.getAttribute('data-tp');
                    if (tp === 'yes') dismiss(true);
                    if (tp === 'no' || tp === 'close') dismiss(false);
                });

                document.body.appendChild(overlay);
            };

            window._tpPushConsentQueue = function(detail) {
                var consented = false;
                try { consented = localStorage.getItem('consent_sidebar') === 'granted'; } catch(e) {}
                if (consented) {
                    window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('tp-open-sidebar', { detail: detail }));
                    return;
                }

                var handled = false;

                var fallbackTimer = setTimeout(function() {
                    if (!handled) {
                        handled = true;
                        window._tpShowConsentModal(detail, function() {
                            window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('tp-open-sidebar', { detail: detail }));
                        });
                    }
                }, 8000);

                if (typeof window.__tcfapi === 'function') {
                    window.__tcfapi('addEventListener', 2, function(tcData, success) {
                        if (success && tcData.eventStatus === 'cmpuishown') {
                            clearTimeout(fallbackTimer);
                            window.__tcfapi('removeEventListener', 2, function() {}, tcData.listenerId);
                        }
                    });
                }

                window.GRVConsentQueue = window.GRVConsentQueue || [];
                window.GRVConsentQueue.push(function(d) {
                    if (handled) return;
                    handled = true;
                    clearTimeout(fallbackTimer);

                    if (!(d.user && d.user.isLoggedIn() && d.user.hasValidSubscription())) {
                        window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('tp-open-sidebar', { detail: detail }));
                    } else {
                        window._tpShowConsentModal(detail, function() {
                            window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('tp-open-sidebar', { detail: detail }));
                        });
                    }
                });
            };

            // PART 3: Auto-open sidebar on TalkingPoints story pages
            function checkHashAndOpenSidebar() {
                const hashParams = new URLSearchParams(window.location.hash.substring(1));
                const tpopen = hashParams.get('tpopen');
                const tp = hashParams.get('tp');
                const commentID = hashParams.get('commentID') || hashParams.get('commentId');

                // Extract slug from URL path if we're on a TalkingPoints story page
                const path = window.location.pathname;

                // Check if this is an IB page (/talkingpoints/{tp-slug}/{ib-slug}/)
                const ibMatch = path.match(/^\/talkingpoints\/([^\/]+)\/([^\/]+)\/?$/);
                if (ibMatch) {
                    // IB pages have their own inline script that dispatches tp-open-sidebar
                    return;
                }

                // Story landing pages (/talkingpoints/{slug}/) have their own inline script that dispatches tp-open-sidebar.
                const tpMatch = path.match(/^\/talkingpoints\/([^\/]+)\/?$/);
                if (tpMatch) {
                    return;
                }

                // Only handle explicit tpopen=true with tp param
                if (tpopen === 'true' && tp) {
                    // Validate story exists via API first
                    const storyDataUrl = new URL("https:\/\/www.hitc.com\/json\/talking-points\/v1\/story-data");
                    storyDataUrl.searchParams.set('tp', tp);
                    fetch(storyDataUrl.toString())
                        .then(response => response.json())
                        .then(data => {
                            if (data.found && data.storyId) {
                                window._tpPushConsentQueue({
                                    storyId: data.storyId || '',
                                    storyName: data.storyName || tp,
                                    storySlug: tp,
                                    commentId: commentID || '',
                                    pinnedIbId: data.pinnedIbId || '',
                                });
                            }
                        })
                        .catch(error => {
                            // Silently fail if API validation fails
                        });
                }
            }

            // Check on page load
            function initHashCheck() {
                // Check immediately
                checkHashAndOpenSidebar();
                // Also check after a short delay to ensure Vue components are initialized
                setTimeout(checkHashAndOpenSidebar, 500);
            }

            if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
                document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', initHashCheck);
            } else {
                initHashCheck();
            }

            // Check on hash change
            window.addEventListener('hashchange', checkHashAndOpenSidebar);

            // PART 4: Attach click handlers when sidebar opens (for story pages)
            // This ensures handlers work even when switching stories via dropdown
            window.addEventListener('tp-open-sidebar', function() {
                // Wait for sidebar to render
                setTimeout(function() {
                    attachStoryPageHandlers();
                }, 500);
            });

            function attachStoryPageHandlers() {
                // Only attach handlers if we're on a story or IB page
                const path = window.location.pathname;
                const tpMatch = path.match(/^\/talkingpoints\/([^\/]+)\/?$/);
                const ibMatch = path.match(/^\/talkingpoints\/([^\/]+)\/([^\/]+)\/?$/);
                if (!tpMatch && !ibMatch) return;

                var backUrl = window.location.origin + '/talkingpoints';

                // Attach close button handler and swap icon to back arrow
                const closeButton = document.querySelector('.vue-tp-close-button');
                if (closeButton && !closeButton.hasAttribute('data-tp-back-handler')) {
                    closeButton.setAttribute('data-tp-back-handler', 'true');
                    closeButton.innerHTML = '<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="16" height="16" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><line x1="20" y1="12" x2="4" y2="12"/><polyline points="10 18 4 12 10 6"/></svg>';
                    closeButton.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
                        e.preventDefault();
                        e.stopPropagation();
                        window.location.href = backUrl;
                    }, true);
                }

                // Attach outside sidebar area handler
                const outsideArea = document.querySelector('.vue-tp-outside-sidebar-area');
                if (outsideArea && !outsideArea.hasAttribute('data-tp-back-handler')) {
                    outsideArea.setAttribute('data-tp-back-handler', 'true');
                    outsideArea.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
                        e.preventDefault();
                        e.stopPropagation();
                        window.location.href = backUrl;
                    }, true);
                }
            }

            // Listen for sidebar close event on story and IB pages
            window.addEventListener('tp-sidebar-closed', function() {
                const path = window.location.pathname;
                if (path.match(/^\/talkingpoints\/[^\/]+/)) {
                    window.location.href = window.location.origin + '/talkingpoints';
                }
            });
        })();
    </script> <script>window.GRVNewsLetterURL="\/json\/newsletter\/echobox\/submit"</script> <script>    window.addEventListener('message', updateProductHeight, false);

      return new Promise((resolve) => {
        if (window.grvAuth) {
          return resolve(window.grvAuth);
        }
        const interval = setInterval(() => {
          if (window.grvAuth) {
            clearInterval(interval);
            resolve(window.grvAuth);
          }
        }, 50);
      });
    }

    function showTPCTA(isSignedIn) {
      /** @type {NodeListOf<HTMLElement>} */
      const joinTexts = document.querySelectorAll('.grv-talking-point__join-text');
      joinTexts.forEach(el => el.style.display = isSignedIn ? 'flex' : 'none');
      /** @type {NodeListOf<HTMLElement>} */
      const signInCtas = document.querySelectorAll('.grv-talking-point__sign-in-cta');
      signInCtas.forEach(el => el.style.display = isSignedIn ? 'none' : 'flex');
    }

    (async function () {
      const grvAuth = await waitForGrvAuth();
      await grvAuth.ready;
      const session = await grvAuth.getSession();
      showTPCTA(!!session?.user);
    })();
